description: Machine-queryable evidence-strength registry. Each claim records its status, confidence, supporting files, what additional evidence would upgrade it, and any known open counterexamples. Statuses: verified_static (reproducible from committed/static artifacts), runtime_observed (seen in a native D3D12 capture), inferred (derived indirectly), unresolved (no evidence gathered).

claims.description: Six weight blobs of 131072 bytes each are extractable from amd_fidelityfx_upscaler_dx12.dll at the documented RVAs and reproduce byte-for-byte across re-extraction.
claims.needs: Runtime confirmation that the same bytes are bound as an InitializerBuffer during dispatch.

claims.description: The five standard presets (quality, balanced, performance, ultraperf, native) are byte-identical; the DRS preset is a separately trained network (96.1% byte difference).
claims.needs: Runtime confirmation that preset selection does not load a different network.

claims.description: The DXIL corpus contains exactly 27 model entry points (prepass, pass1..pass12, pass0_post..pass12_post, postpass) with no pass13.
claims.needs: Runtime capture correlating PSO CS bytecode hashes to these entry points.

claims.description: Static dispatch runs a 27-iteration loop with optional SPD AutoExposure before and RCAS/Debug View after.
claims.needs: Native D3D12 capture showing the actual per-frame dispatch sequence and loop count.
claims.open_counterexamples: Conditional flags may reorder or suppress passes at runtime; static order is the unconditional path.

claims.description: The 4.0.2-derived 78-tensor offset map parses 4.1.0 blob regions into plausible typed values.
claims.needs: Runtime offset-use confirmation; current check is a plausibility parse, not a proof of runtime addressing.
claims.open_counterexamples: Plausibility does not prove the runtime actually reads these exact offsets.

claims.description: The network is a bottleneck architecture with symmetric pass structure; per-pass kernels are consistent with 3x3/4x4/5x4 from phi-node counts.
claims.needs: Runtime tensor-shape capture; attention mechanisms cannot be ruled out under integer-only computation.
claims.open_counterexamples: U-Net label unsupported (no skip-connection evidence); kernel sizes inferred not extracted; attention not excluded.

claims.description: The runtime per-frame dispatch order matches the static 27-loop analysis.
claims.needs: Native Windows D3D12 capture conforming to runtime-validation/schema.json.
claims.open_counterexamples: All three Linux/Proton capture methods failed; no runtime data exists.

claims.description: Per-pass constant-buffer values at runtime match the static 5/6-register (80/96-byte) layout.
claims.needs: CBV hex dumps from a native capture; current evidence is register-index access only, not values.

claims.description: The data DLL can be rebuilt from RE'd source + extracted blobs and its data sections compared against the original per-region.
claims.needs: Byte-equality is not claimed; runtime API-equivalence test against the real provider DLL.
claims.open_counterexamples: Per-section hash comparison, not a byte-equal independent rebuild (the copy-bytes proof was removed as circular).

claims.description: Each 4.1.0 blob contains an 888-byte region of 222 finite FP32 values not present in 4.0.2, followed by 96 bytes of zero padding.
claims.needs: Runtime confirmation these are output biases and consumed as FP32.

claims.description: Weights use an FP8 codebook decoded through a 256-entry atomicCompareExchange table with 0x100 stride; the mechanism appears to function as a side-effect-free table read.
claims.needs: Runtime confirmation the atomic calls are reads, not stateful writes; rule out compiler-artifact and mixed read-write explanations.
claims.open_counterexamples: Alternative explanations not ruled out: persistent atomic workspace, compiler artifact, or mixed read/write.
